evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/httpx. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/httpx --head qlty-setup --base master
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Python project using ruff. Detects existing pyproject.toml with ruff config. Enables ruff, trufflehog, markdownlint, yamllint, actionlint. Asks user about modes and smells before committing.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/httpx
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
ruff plugin is enabled (Python project)
trufflehog plugin is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
Detected existing pyproject.toml with ruff configuration and referenced it via config_files or noted it is auto-detected
Did NOT enable flake8 or black (ruff covers both)
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream encode/httpx repo

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is at laura-mlg/qlty-test-mono. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/qlty-test-mono --head qlty-setup --base main

This is a fullstack monorepo with three subdirectories: frontend/ (React + TypeScript, has .eslintrc.js and package.json with all @typescript-eslint and react eslint deps already listed in devDependencies), backend/ (Python FastAPI, has pyproject.toml with [tool.ruff] and [tool.mypy]), and infra/ (Terraform). Each subdir has its own language stack. Set up qlty.toml with appropriate prefix scoping. For the frontend eslint plugin, use package_file pointing to frontend/package.json with package_filters rather than duplicating extra_packages. Include tsc for TypeScript type checking (tsconfig.json present in frontend/).
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a TypeScript+Python+Terraform monorepo. Uses prefix scoping for each subdirectory. Frontend eslint uses package_file with package_filters. Separate ruff and mypy for backend. checkov and tflint for infra.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/qlty-test-mono
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
eslint plugin has prefix = "frontend" to scope it to the frontend subdirectory
eslint plugin uses package_file referencing frontend/package.json (not extra_packages) because devDependencies already has all needed packages
eslint plugin uses package_filters to limit which packages are installed
ruff plugin is scoped to the backend/ subdirectory via prefix or [[exclude]]
tflint or checkov is scoped to the infra/ subdirectory
trufflehog is enabled globally (not scoped)
tsc is included for TypeScript type checking
Security plugins (trufflehog, osv-scanner or trivy) are set to block mode

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is at laura-mlg/qlty-test-data-science. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/qlty-test-data-science --head qlty-setup --base main

This is a Python data science repo. The code has functions with 6-8 parameters (train_model, preprocess_features, evaluate_model) which is normal for ML pipelines, and has three structurally identical summarize_*_data functions (train/test/validation) which is an intentional pattern for separate dataset stats. Set up qlty.toml with smells configuration appropriate for data science code: raise function_parameters threshold, adjust function_complexity, and handle the intentional duplication.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Python data science project. Smells section includes raised thresholds appropriate for ML code. Uses language.python.smells overrides or global threshold adjustments. Either disables identical_code or raises duplication threshold.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/qlty-test-data-science
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
ruff is enabled (Python project with pyproject.toml [tool.ruff])
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
smells section is present
function_parameters threshold is raised above the default of 4 (either via [language.python.smells] or [smells.function_parameters]) to accommodate ML functions with many params
Either smells.identical_code.enabled = false OR smells.duplication.nodes_threshold is raised, to account for the intentionally identical summarize_train/test/validation_data functions
Did NOT enable both ruff and flake8

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/puma. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/puma --head qlty-setup-v2 --base main

This is the Puma Ruby web server. It has a Gemfile that already declares rubocop and rubocop-performance gems. It also has .rubocop.yml and .rubocop_todo.yml. Configure qlty.toml to use package_file pointing to the Gemfile (with package_filters) rather than extra_packages, since rubocop extensions are already declared there. Reference both .rubocop.yml and .rubocop_todo.yml in config_files. Add [[triage]] rules to downgrade known-noisy auto-correctable rubocop Style offenses to monitor mode.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Ruby project. Rubocop plugin uses package_file = "Gemfile" with package_filters instead of extra_packages. Both .rubocop.yml and .rubocop_todo.yml referenced in config_files. [[triage]] block downgrade noisy rubocop style rules.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/puma
PR branch is qlty-setup-v2
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
rubocop plugin is enabled
rubocop plugin uses package_file = "Gemfile" (not extra_packages) since rubocop and rubocop-performance are already in the Gemfile
rubocop plugin uses package_filters to limit which gems are installed
rubocop plugin has config_files referencing .rubocop.yml
At least one [[triage]] block is present to handle noisy rubocop rules (e.g., Style/* rules set to monitor)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
Did NOT use extra_packages for rubocop extensions since Gemfile already declares them

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/spring-petclinic. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/spring-petclinic --head qlty-setup-v2 --base main

This is a Java Spring Boot application. It uses checkstyle (via Maven plugin in pom.xml). It also has docker-compose.yml and k8s/ directory with Kubernetes manifests. Set up qlty.toml with: google-java-format and pmd for Java code quality; checkov for the Docker and K8s files; kube-linter for the k8s/ manifests; smells with per-language Java overrides (Java enterprise code typically needs higher complexity thresholds); and [[triage]] rules to handle known-noisy pmd findings.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Java Spring Boot project. Includes google-java-format, pmd, checkov for Docker/K8s, kube-linter. Java smells thresholds adjusted. [[triage]] present for noisy pmd rules.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/spring-petclinic
PR branch is qlty-setup-v2
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
google-java-format is enabled for Java formatting
pmd is enabled for Java static analysis
checkov is enabled (docker-compose.yml and k8s manifests present) and set to block mode
kube-linter is enabled (k8s/ directory with Kubernetes manifests present)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
smells section is present with Java-appropriate threshold adjustments (function_complexity > 15 or language.java.smells override)
At least one [[triage]] block is present (e.g., to downgrade noisy pmd rules to monitor)
yamllint is enabled (YAML files present including docker-compose.yml and k8s manifests)

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/terraform-aws-vpc. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/terraform-aws-vpc --head qlty-setup-v2 --base master

This is a Terraform module for AWS VPC. It has GitHub Actions workflows. It uses tflint in its pre-commit config. Set up qlty.toml with: terraform for formatting; tflint for linting; trivy with both config and vuln drivers (IaC scanning + vulnerability scanning); checkov for IaC security, with affects_cache to ensure re-analysis when the Terraform files change; [[triage]] to handle known AWS-specific IaC findings that are intentional patterns in a reusable module; and actionlint + yamllint for the GitHub Actions workflows.
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Terraform module. trivy uses drivers = ["config", "vuln"]. checkov has affects_cache. [[triage]] handles AWS module patterns. actionlint and yamllint enabled.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/terraform-aws-vpc
PR branch is qlty-setup-v2
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
terraform plugin is enabled for formatting
tflint plugin is enabled
trivy plugin is enabled with drivers that include "config" for IaC scanning
trivy drivers list does NOT include "fs-vuln" (invalid value)
checkov plugin is enabled and set to block mode
checkov plugin has affects_cache listing relevant Terraform config files
At least one [[triage]] block is present for IaC findings
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
yamllint is enabled (YAML files present)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This is a dedicated test repo at laura-mlg/qlty-test-tsc. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/qlty-test-tsc --head qlty-setup --base main. Note: do NOT enable the tsc plugin — tsc causes build errors in Qlty Cloud on most repos. Instead demonstrate skip_upstream conceptually and focus on affects_cache + .qlty/configs/ storage.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml using affects_cache=["tsconfig.json"] on eslint, config stored in .qlty/configs/.eslintrc.js, skip_upstream documented in comments. No tsc plugin.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/qlty-test-tsc with branch qlty-setup
eslint plugin has affects_cache = ["tsconfig.json"]
Config file stored in .qlty/configs/ and referenced via config_files
Uses extra_packages with @typescript-eslint (v5 or v6 only, not v7+)
Does NOT include tsc plugin (known to cause build errors in Qlty Cloud)
Security plugins set to block mode

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a test repo at laura-mlg/qlty-test-smells. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/qlty-test-smells --head qlty-setup --base main. [[triage]] block ignores rubocop Metrics/ParameterLists in test files
Uses [[plugin]] blocks (not [plugins.enabled_plugins.X] format)

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/devise. Default branch is main. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/devise --head qlty-setup --base main.

devise is a Ruby on Rails authentication gem with a dummy Rails app in spec/test_app. The repo has a .reek.yml for code smell configuration. Configure qlty.toml with: rubocop for linting (use Gemfile as package_file with package_filters for rubocop gems); brakeman for Rails security scanning (the dummy app in spec/ makes this a Rails project); reek for Ruby code smell detection using the .reek.yml config. Add [[triage]] to suppress brakeman findings in spec/ test fixtures. Rails-specific: brakeman should be set to block mode as a security scanner.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml enabling rubocop (Gemfile package_file), brakeman (Rails security, block mode), reek (.reek.yml config_files), [[triage]] suppressing brakeman in test directories.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/devise with branch qlty-setup
rubocop plugin is enabled
brakeman plugin is enabled and set to block mode (security scanner for Rails)
reek plugin is enabled with config_files = [".reek.yml"]
At least one [[triage]] block suppresses brakeman findings in spec/ or test/ directories
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
Did NOT enable standardrb (rubocop already present)

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/hono. Default branch is main. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/hono --head qlty-setup --base main.

hono is a TypeScript web framework. The repo has biome.json (added for this eval), eslint config, and prettier. When biome.json is present, use ONLY biome — do NOT add eslint or prettier. The biome.json covers both linting and formatting. Configure qlty.toml with biome (covering both lint and format), trufflehog, markdownlint, actionlint, yamllint.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml using ONLY biome for JS/TS linting+formatting. No eslint, no prettier. biome is the exclusive JS/TS tool when biome.json is detected.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/hono with branch qlty-setup
biome plugin is enabled
eslint plugin is NOT enabled (biome.json present — biome is exclusive)
prettier plugin is NOT enabled (biome handles formatting)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
yamllint is enabled

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/sidekiq. Default branch is main. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/sidekiq --head qlty-setup --base main.

sidekiq is a popular Ruby background job library. It has a .rubocop.yml config and rubocop extensions in its Gemfile (rubocop-performance, rubocop-minitest). It also has a .reek.yml added for code smell detection. The team uses rubocop for LINTING ONLY — not as a formatter (they use a separate formatter). To achieve linting-only behavior: use [[triage]] blocks to set all rubocop Style/* and Layout/* rules to monitor mode (treating them as formatting noise, not linting issues). Separate rubocop linting rules (Lint/*, Performance/*, Security/*) should stay at comment mode. Also enable reek for smell detection.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml: rubocop with Gemfile package_file, [[triage]] moving Style/* and Layout/* cop categories to monitor, reek with .reek.yml config_files. Demonstrates rubocop as linter-only via triage.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/sidekiq with branch qlty-setup
rubocop plugin is enabled
rubocop plugin uses package_file = "Gemfile" or extra_packages listing rubocop extensions
At least one [[triage]] block targets rubocop Style/* or Layout/* rules and sets mode to monitor (linting-only approach)
reek plugin is enabled with config_files = [".reek.yml"]
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/flask. Default branch is main. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/flask --head qlty-setup --base main.

flask is a Python web framework. It has pyproject.toml with [tool.ruff] config and a .semgrep.yaml with custom Flask security rules (added for this eval). Configure qlty.toml with: ruff for linting/formatting; bandit for Python security analysis set to block mode; semgrep with config_files = [".semgrep.yaml"] to run custom rules; osv-scanner for dependency scanning. Use [[triage]] to suppress specific bandit false-positive rules (e.g., B104 hardcoded binding to all interfaces) that are known noise in a web framework.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml enabling ruff, bandit (block), semgrep (config_files pointing to .semgrep.yaml), osv-scanner. [[triage]] suppresses specific noisy bandit rules.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/flask with branch qlty-setup
ruff plugin is enabled (Python project with pyproject.toml [tool.ruff])
bandit plugin is enabled and set to block mode (security-sensitive framework)
semgrep plugin is enabled with config_files = [".semgrep.yaml"]
osv-scanner or trivy is enabled for dependency scanning
At least one [[triage]] block suppresses a specific noisy bandit rule to monitor mode
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
Did NOT enable both ruff and flake8

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/flysystem. Default branch is 3.x. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/flysystem --head qlty-setup --base 3.x.

flysystem is a PHP filesystem abstraction library. It has both phpstan.neon AND .php-cs-fixer.dist.php config files. Configure qlty.toml with: php-cs-fixer for formatting (NOT php-codesniffer — .php-cs-fixer.dist.php takes precedence); phpstan for static analysis using config_files = ["phpstan.neon"]. phpstan requires phpstan/phpstan-strict-rules as an extra_package (used via phpstan.neon extends). Use [[triage]] to downgrade a phpstan rule about mixed types to monitor mode for this first-time setup.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml: php-cs-fixer (not php-codesniffer), phpstan with config_files + extra_packages for phpstan extensions, [[triage]] for phpstan mixed-type rule.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/flysystem with branch qlty-setup on base 3.x
php-cs-fixer plugin is enabled for PHP formatting
php-codesniffer is NOT enabled (.php-cs-fixer.dist.php detected — use php-cs-fixer)
phpstan plugin is enabled with config_files = ["phpstan.neon"]
phpstan plugin has extra_packages listing at least one phpstan extension (e.g., phpstan/phpstan-strict-rules)
At least one [[triage]] block targets a phpstan rule
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/gin. Default branch is master. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/gin --head qlty-setup --base master.

gin is a Go web framework with a .golangci.yml that enables 15+ linters (asciicheck, copyloopvar, errorlint, gosec, revive, testifylint, and more). Configure qlty.toml with: golangci-lint (config_files referencing .golangci.yml), version pinned to "1.59.1" for stability; osv-scanner for Go module dependency scanning. Use [[triage]] blocks to: downgrade gosec findings to monitor mode (for first-run noise reduction) and promote revive errors to block mode (team enforces revive).
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml: golangci-lint with config_files, version pin, [[triage]] downgrading gosec to monitor and promoting revive to block. osv-scanner enabled.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/gin with branch qlty-setup
golangci-lint plugin is enabled with config_files = [".golangci.yml"]
golangci-lint plugin has version = "1.59.1" (or another pinned version)
gofmt is NOT separately enabled (golangci-lint wraps it)
osv-scanner or trivy is enabled for dependency scanning
At least one [[triage]] block downgrading gosec findings (e.g., to monitor)
At least one [[triage]] block promoting revive findings (e.g., to block or comment)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/commons-lang. Default branch is master. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/commons-lang --head qlty-setup --base master.

apache/commons-lang is a Java utility library with checkstyle configuration in src/conf/checkstyle/. Configure qlty.toml with: google-java-format for formatting; pmd for static analysis (set to monitor initially — noisy on first run); radarlint-java for deep code quality analysis in comment mode. Java-specific smells: enterprise Java typically has higher complexity — set language.java.smells function_complexity threshold to 20 and function_parameters to 6. Use [[triage]] to suppress a specific pmd rule (e.g., UselessParentheses or ExcessiveImports) that is known to be noisy.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml: google-java-format, pmd (monitor), radarlint-java (comment), [language.java.smells] thresholds, [[triage]] for noisy pmd rules.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/commons-lang with branch qlty-setup
google-java-format plugin is enabled for Java formatting
pmd plugin is enabled, set to monitor mode (noisy first-run strategy)
radarlint-java plugin is enabled in comment or monitor mode
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
[language.java.smells] section is present with function_complexity threshold above 15
At least one [[triage]] block suppresses a specific noisy pmd rule
exclude_patterns includes target/ (Maven build output)
markdownlint is enabled

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is at laura-mlg/qlty-test-ts-multi-eslint. Default branch is main. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/qlty-test-ts-multi-eslint --head qlty-setup --base main.

This is a TypeScript npm workspace monorepo with two packages needing SEPARATE eslint configurations:
- packages/ui/ — React components with its own .eslintrc.js using eslint-plugin-react, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, @typescript-eslint
- packages/api/ — Node.js API with its own .eslintrc.js using eslint-plugin-import, @typescript-eslint strict type-checking rules

Configure qlty.toml with TWO separate eslint [[plugin]] blocks: one scoped with prefix="packages/ui" referencing packages/ui/.eslintrc.js and packages/ui/package.json for extra_packages; one scoped with prefix="packages/api" referencing packages/api/.eslintrc.js and packages/api/package.json. Both use @typescript-eslint at v6.x (never v7+). Include trufflehog globally.
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml containing two [[plugin]] name="eslint" blocks: one with prefix=packages/ui + config_files + extra_packages for React deps; one with prefix=packages/api + config_files + extra_packages for API deps. Demonstrates per-package eslint scoping.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/qlty-test-ts-multi-eslint with branch qlty-setup
Two [[plugin]] blocks both with name = "eslint" present in qlty.toml
One eslint block has prefix = "packages/ui" (or similar) scoping it to the UI package
One eslint block has prefix = "packages/api" (or similar) scoping it to the API package
Each eslint block has config_files referencing the respective .eslintrc.js
extra_packages for UI block includes eslint-plugin-react and @typescript-eslint at v6.x
extra_packages for API block includes eslint-plugin-import and @typescript-eslint at v6.x
@typescript-eslint packages are pinned to v6.x (NOT v7+)
trufflehog is enabled globally (not scoped)

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/serde. Default branch is master. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/serde --head qlty-setup --base master.

serde is a Rust serialization framework structured as a workspace with 5 crates: serde/, serde_core/, serde_derive/, serde_derive_internals/, test_suite/. A single qlty.toml config at the workspace root should cover all crates — do NOT scope by prefix. Configure qlty.toml with: clippy for linting; rustfmt for formatting; osv-scanner for Cargo.lock dependency scanning. Use [[triage]] to: downgrade clippy::complexity lint category findings to monitor mode (too noisy for a mature library); promote clippy::correctness to block mode (correctness issues should always be fixed).
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml: clippy + rustfmt at workspace root covering all crates (no prefix). osv-scanner. [[triage]] downgrading clippy complexity to monitor, promoting correctness to block.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/serde with branch qlty-setup
clippy plugin is enabled (Rust workspace)
rustfmt plugin is enabled
No prefix scoping on clippy or rustfmt (single config covers all workspace crates)
osv-scanner or trivy is enabled for Cargo.lock dependency scanning
At least one [[triage]] block targets clippy complexity or pedantic lints (downgrade to monitor)
At least one [[triage]] block promotes clippy correctness findings to block mode
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
exclude_patterns includes target/ (Rust build output)

evals.prompt:
IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/mkdocs. Default branch is master. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/mkdocs --head qlty-setup --base master.

mkdocs is a Python documentation tool with extensive Markdown documentation in docs/. It has a .vale.ini added for prose quality checking. Configure qlty.toml with: ruff for Python linting (pyproject.toml present with [tool.ruff]); vale for prose quality in docs/ with config_files = [".vale.ini"]; markdownlint with config_files if a markdownlint config is found; yamllint (YAML config files present); bandit for Python security. For vale: set mode to monitor initially as prose style rules are team-preference. Use [[triage]] to limit vale to only run on docs/ markdown files (not generated output).
evals.expected_output: PR with qlty.toml: ruff, vale (config_files=.vale.ini, monitor mode), markdownlint, yamllint, bandit. [[triage]] or [[exclude]] limiting vale to docs/ only.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/mkdocs with branch qlty-setup
ruff plugin is enabled (Python project with pyproject.toml [tool.ruff])
vale plugin is enabled with config_files = [".vale.ini"]
vale is set to monitor mode (prose style is preference-based)
markdownlint is enabled (extensive markdown docs present)
yamllint is enabled (YAML config files present)
bandit is enabled or proposed (Python project, docs tool)
At least one [[triage]] or [[exclude]] block scopes vale to docs/ directory
trufflehog is enabled and set to block mode
